serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one bowl (approximately 450 g), as listed on the GrabFood platform for this dish.

nutrient_source.title: Health Promotion Board Singapore — Nutrient Composition of Food (HPB Foodo / Singapore Food Composition Database)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are estimated by component-level aggregation using HPB Singapore Food Composition Database entries for roasted chicken, white rice, roasted potatoes, corn, tomatoes, lettuce, spring onions, and soy-based dressing. No single HPB entry exists for this exact composite dish. Values from the input record (provenance: ai_generated, confidence 0.7) are retained and cross-referenced against HPB component data; they are consistent with expected ranges for a rice bowl of this composition. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used as secondary cross-check for individual ingredient macros.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; and University of Sydney GI Database — White rice (boiled, jasmine/fragrant) analogous estimate
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this composite Chicken Everyday Bowl. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the dominant carbohydrate contributor being white jasmine/fragrant rice (GI ~64–72 per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care). The presence of roasted chicken protein, fat from dressing, and dietary fibre from vegetables (1.33 g/100g) moderates the overall GI slightly below pure white rice. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (65 × 55.0 g carbohydrate in 450 g serving) / 100 = 35.8. Available carbohydrate per serving: 12.22 g/100g × 4.5 = 54.99 g ≈ 55.0 g.

hypertension.guidance: Sodium at 188.9 mg/100g is below 200 mg/100g per 100 g, but a full 450 g serving delivers approximately 850 mg sodium — nearly 37% of the 2,300 mg daily limit — and the soy vinaigrette and house-made dressing are variable sodium sources that can substantially increase this load; patients with hypertension should request dressings on the side and use them sparingly.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI white rice (GI ~65) with low dietary fibre (1.33 g/100g); 55 g available carbohydrate per serving; GL 35.8
type2_diabetes.guidance: The dominant carbohydrate source is white jasmine rice (GI ~65, GL 35.8 per serving), and dietary fibre is low at 1.33 g/100g, providing insufficient buffering of post-meal glucose excursions; patients with type 2 diabetes should request a brown rice or cauliflower rice substitution, reduce the potato portion, and increase the lettuce and vegetable components to lower the glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.89 g/100g), low trans fat (0.02 g/100g), moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Saturated fat is low at 0.89 g/100g and trans fat is minimal at 0.02 g/100g, supporting a heart-healthy fat profile; however, the cumulative sodium per serving (~850 mg) warrants attention for patients with cardiovascular disease, and dressing portions should be controlled to keep total sodium within recommended limits.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Nightshade vegetables (tomatoes, potatoes); low anti-inflammatory nutrient density
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Tomatoes and potatoes are nightshade vegetables that may exacerbate symptoms in nightshade-sensitive individuals with rheumatoid arthritis; patients should request tomato- and potato-free preparation, and the absence of omega-3 fatty acids, cruciferous vegetables, or anti-inflammatory spices means this dish provides no active anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Chicken Everyday Bowl is a contemporary Singaporean rice bowl dish available on the GrabFood delivery platform. It consists of roasted chicken pieces served over fragrant white jasmine rice that has been tossed in a light soy vinaigrette, accompanied by roasted potatoes, fresh corn kernels, sliced tomatoes, shredded lettuce, and spring onions, finished with a signature house-made dressing. The dish is assembled rather than cooked as a single unit, combining warm roasted proteins and starches with fresh raw vegetables. The flavour profile is savoury and lightly tangy from the soy vinaigrette, with the roasted chicken providing umami depth and the fresh vegetables contributing crunch and brightness. A standard serving is approximately 450 g. Nutritionally, the dish provides 122 kcal per 100 g, with 7.78 g protein, 4.44 g fat (0.89 g saturated), and 12.22 g carbohydrate per 100 g. Sodium is 188.9 mg/100g, and dietary fibre is 1.33 g/100g. The dish is moderate in glycaemic index (~65) due to the white rice base, with a glycaemic load of approximately 35.8 per serving, making it a concern for type 2 diabetes management. It is suitable for halal diets and is dairy-free, but contains soy allergens from the vinaigrette. The dish is not suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes without modification (brown rice substitution recommended), and sodium management is important for hypertension patients given the variable dressing contribution. Cardiovascular risk is low due to minimal saturated and trans fat content.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from an ai_generated input record with confidence 0.7, cross-referenced against HPB Singapore Food Composition Database component entries for the individual ingredients (roasted chicken, white rice, potatoes, corn, tomatoes, lettuce, spring onions, soy dressing). No single approved-source entry exists for this exact composite dish. Values are internally consistent and plausible for a rice bowl of this composition. GI is an analogous estimate based on the white jasmine rice dominant carbohydrate source from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dish is not available in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database or USDA FoodData Central for this specific assembled bowl preparation as of 2024. 2. potassium_mg is set to -1: potassium data for this composite dish is not directly available in the HPB database entry used; estimated ~290 mg/100g from component analysis (corn, lettuce, tomatoes, spring onions) but not confirmed by a single approved-source entry and therefore not populated as a numeric value. 3. vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g are set to -1: these micronutrient values for this specific composite dish are not available as a single composite entry in the HPB Singapore Food Composition Database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2024. 4. The input record listed 'primary_cuisine' as 'Vegetarian Friendly' which is a dietary tag, not a cuisine classification; this has been corrected to 'Singaporean / Contemporary Asian' based on country_of_origin: Singapore and the dish composition. 5. The dish contains chicken and is therefore not vegetarian despite the input ethnic_tags; dietary_flags.is_vegetarian is set to false. 6. trans_fat_g is retained at 0.02 g/100g as reported in the input; this is a trace level consistent with minor trans fat from the soy vinaigrette or dressing. 7. GI is an analogous estimate (GI ~65, medium) based on white jasmine rice as the dominant carbohydrate source per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish.
